South Manganese Investment Limited ( (HK:1091) ) just unveiled an announcement.

South Manganese Investment Limited has disclosed that substantial shareholder Mr. Cheung Ming has sold his entire stake of 534,691,800 shares, representing about 10.83% of the company’s issued share capital, at an average price of HK$0.24 per share, and as a result no longer holds any shares in the company. The board stated that this disposal is not expected to have a material impact on the group’s business operations or financial position, suggesting that while the move significantly alters the shareholder structure, it should not affect day-to-day activities or the company’s immediate financial health.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1091) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.34 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on South Manganese Investment Limited stock, see the HK:1091 Stock Forecast page.

The company is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical corporate governance framework for a Hong Kong–listed issuer.

data.title: Suzhou Novosense Forecasts 70% Revenue Jump and Narrowing Losses for 2025
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Suzhou Novosense Microlectronics Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2676) ).

Suzhou Novosense Microelectronics has issued a positive profit alert for 2025, flagging a sharp rebound in operating performance despite remaining loss-making. The group expects revenue to surge 68%–73% year-on-year to between RMB3.3 billion and RMB3.4 billion, driven by sustained growth in automotive electronics sales, recovering demand in energy and industrial automation, and strong AI-related demand for server power products, alongside incremental contributions from the newly consolidated MagnTek business. Net loss attributable to shareholders is forecast to narrow to RMB200 million–RMB250 million in 2025, from RMB402.9 million in 2024, with a similar reduction in losses after excluding non-recurring items, underscoring improved scale and operating leverage that could bolster investor confidence in the company’s path toward profitability.

More about Suzhou Novosense Microlectronics Co. Ltd. Class H

Suzhou Novosense Microelectronics Co., Ltd. is a China-based semiconductor company focused on microelectronics products, with a growing presence in automotive electronics, energy, industrial automation, and server power applications. The consolidation of Shanghai MagnTek Microelectronics Co., Ltd. has further expanded its product portfolio and strengthened its positioning in these downstream markets, particularly as demand recovers in photovoltaics, energy storage, and AI-driven server power sectors.

Current Market Cap: HK$31.14B

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

China Renewable Energy Investment ( (HK:0987) ) has issued an update.

China Renewable Energy Investment Limited has formalised detailed terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, strengthening its corporate governance framework and ensuring greater transparency in board appointments. The revised structure requires a majority of independent non-executive directors on the committee, mandates at least one director of a different gender, and sets out clear rules on meetings, quorum, voting procedures and minute-keeping. The committee is tasked with annually reviewing the board’s structure, size, composition and diversity, identifying and recommending candidates for directorships, assessing the independence of independent directors, and overseeing succession planning for the chairman and chief executive. It will also monitor the implementation and disclosure of the company’s board diversity and nomination policies, signalling a more rigorous approach to governance and diversity that aligns with market and regulatory expectations and may enhance confidence among shareholders and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0987) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.13 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Renewable Energy Investment stock, see the HK:0987 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Renewable Energy Investment

China Renewable Energy Investment Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company focused on the renewable energy sector, investing in and operating clean energy assets in China. The group positions itself within the broader transition to low-carbon power, where strong corporate governance and board oversight are increasingly important to institutional investors and other stakeholders.

data.title: Capinfo Discloses Series of BJCA Share Disposals After Compliance Lapse
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Capinfo Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1075) ) has issued an update.

Capinfo Company Limited has disclosed that between 24 November 2025 and 30 December 2025 it disposed of several tranches of BJCA listed shares on the open market, in aggregate reaching a size that constitutes notifiable transactions under Hong Kong’s Listing Rules. The company sold 1,441,600 BJCA shares in a first batch after its 31 October 2025 announcement, raising about RMB44.1 million and reducing its stake in BJCA to 25.70%, and then continued with further disposals later in December via centralized bidding and other on-market methods. Because one or more of the applicable percentage ratios for each batch of disposals exceeded 5% but remained below 25%, the transactions qualified as discloseable transactions subject to reporting and announcement requirements, which the company had initially failed to meet. Capinfo has acknowledged that the first, second and third batches of disposals were not disclosed at the time and stated that it has implemented improvement measures in its internal processes to prevent similar compliance lapses in the future, a move aimed at reassuring regulators and investors about its adherence to listing obligations and governance standards.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1075) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Capinfo Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:1075 Stock Forecast page.

More about Capinfo Co., Ltd. Class H

Capinfo Company Limited is a PRC-incorporated technology and information services provider listed in Hong Kong, engaged in developing and operating digital infrastructure and related IT services, with exposure to the cybersecurity and certification sector through its equity stake in Beijing Certificate Authority (BJCA). The company’s holdings in BJCA form part of its broader portfolio of listed securities, which it actively manages in line with capital allocation and compliance considerations under Hong Kong listing rules.

Beijing SinoHytec Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2402) ) has shared an update.

Beijing SinoHytec Co., Ltd. has issued a preliminary estimate for its 2025 annual results, indicating that net loss attributable to owners of the parent is expected to widen to between RMB550 million and RMB720 million, a year-on-year increase in loss of roughly 20% to 58%, while net loss after deducting non-recurring items is projected at RMB600 million to RMB780 million, up about 11% to 44% from 2024. Shanghai XNG Holdings Limited ( (HK:3666) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shanghai XNG Holdings Limited has issued a quarterly update on actions taken to address its auditor’s prior disclaimer of opinion on the group’s 2024 financial statements, which stemmed from multiple uncertainties related to its ability to continue as a going concern. Since the publication of its annual report in April 2025, the company has applied for a new bank loan, is negotiating the renewal and refinancing of existing bank facilities, and is assessing the feasibility of further equity fundraising in the first quarter of 2026, though several of these initiatives remain in process with no substantial updates yet. Management continues to negotiate with lessors and suppliers and is pursuing cost controls, while also streamlining operations through a rebranding strategy that includes launching lower-cost restaurant formats in higher-spending, trendier locations and considering adjustments to existing outlets, with the stated aim of easing liquidity pressure, improving its financial position, and meeting financial obligations as they fall due.

Shanghai XNG Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating a restaurant business that is currently undergoing a strategic rebranding. The group is focusing on opening new restaurants with lower overhead costs in locations that attract trendier customers with higher spending power, while considering adjustments to parts of its existing restaurant operations to better align with changing consumption trends and patterns.

Zhongguancun Science-Tech Leasing Co., Ltd. has entered into a new set of purchase and finance lease arrangements involving methanol generator sets, continuing its role as buyer from a PRC equipment manufacturer and lessor to an operating lessee in the same sector. Under the latest January 29, 2026 Purchase Agreement II and Finance Lease Agreement III, the company will acquire Leased Assets III for RMB28.35 million and lease them out over 36 months for total payments of about RMB26.78 million, including RMB2.28 million in interest income, complementing two similar 36‑month transactions signed in April and September 2025 with the same lessee and supplier. As these related transactions, all below 5% individually, aggregate to a size exceeding 5% but below 25% under Hong Kong Listing Rules, they are classified as a discloseable transaction requiring notification and announcement, underscoring the growing scale of the company’s methanol generator leasing portfolio and its disciplined use of internal resources to fund asset purchases.

data.title: Ferretti S.p.A. Announces Voluntary Partial Tender Offer and Sets Up Independent Board Committee
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Ferretti S.p.A. ( (IT:YACHT) ).

Ferretti S.p.A. has announced that a shareholder (the Offeror) intends to launch a voluntary conditional partial public tender offer for up to 52,132,861 shares, equivalent to 15.4% of the company’s subscribed and paid-in share capital. If fully taken up, the Offeror’s stake would rise from 14.5% to 29.9%, and the offer will be made to all shareholders on a non-discriminatory basis under the regulatory regimes of both Italy and Hong Kong. In line with takeover rules, Ferretti’s board has formed an Independent Board Committee made up of non-executive and independent directors with no interest in the deal, which will appoint an independent financial adviser to evaluate the offer and guide minority shareholders. Until the company issues a formal response document containing the views of the board, the committee and the adviser, the directors are not making any recommendation and have urged independent shareholders to refrain from taking action on the offer.

More about Ferretti S.p.A.

Ferretti S.p.A. is an Italian joint-stock company with limited liability listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company operates in the luxury yacht and leisure boating industry, focusing on the design, production and sale of high-end vessels for an international clientele and capital markets presence in both Italy and Hong Kong.

Xtep International Holdings ( (HK:1368) ) has provided an announcement.

Xtep International Holdings Limited has announced plans to conduct a concurrent repurchase of its HK$500 million 1.5% convertible bonds due 2026, which are listed on the Vienna Stock Exchange, and to issue a new tranche of convertible bonds to institutional investors. The company has appointed a dealer manager to solicit selling interest from existing bondholders and a manager to coordinate pricing and placement of the new bonds, which will be offered only to institutional investors outside the United States and not to the Hong Kong retail public. Xtep intends to use the net proceeds from the new bond issue primarily to finance the repurchase of the outstanding 2026 convertible bonds, with any remaining funds earmarked for general corporate purposes, effectively refinancing its existing convertible debt and potentially optimizing its capital structure without resorting to an equity offering.

data.title: Hony Media Group Offloads Loss-Making Subsidiary for Nominal HK$1
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Huayi Tencent Entertainment Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0419) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Hony Media Group has agreed to sell its entire equity interest in a wholly owned subsidiary, together with the assignment of a shareholder’s loan of about HK$141.68 million owed by the subsidiary, to independent third party Xu Chun Sheng for a nominal consideration of HK$1, resulting in the subsidiary’s removal from the group’s consolidated financial statements. The deal, which completed on 30 January 2026 and qualifies as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, reflects the subsidiary’s consecutive losses and net liabilities of about HK$6.75 million, and signals the group’s move to shed underperforming assets and clean up its balance sheet, with implications for reducing ongoing financial drag on the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0419) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.36 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Huayi Tencent Entertainment Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:0419 Stock Forecast page.

Hony Media Group, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through various subsidiaries and has been engaged in media-related and investment activities, managing a portfolio of businesses whose financial performance and strategic fit are periodically reviewed.

data.title: Yuexiu Property Restructures Connected Lease for Guangzhou Elderly-Care Facilities
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Yuexiu Property Co ( (HK:0123) ) just unveiled an update.

Yuexiu Property’s subsidiary Guangzhou Haiyiyuan has agreed with Guangzhou Paper, a subsidiary of the group’s ultimate controlling shareholder, to terminate early an existing 12‑year lease on Guangzhou Paper’s properties used for elderly-care services, effective 29 January 2026, and simultaneously enter into a new 20‑year lease starting 30 January 2026 on more favourable terms. Under Hong Kong reporting rules, these arrangements are treated as a disposal and acquisition of right-of-use assets and classified as connected transactions, triggering reporting and announcement requirements but exempting the company from independent shareholders’ approval; due to the unusually long lease term, an independent financial adviser has been appointed to justify the duration as normal business practice, underscoring the company’s commitment to securing long-term, stable access to key elderly-care facilities while complying with governance standards.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0123) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Yuexiu Property Co stock, see the HK:0123 Stock Forecast page.

More about Yuexiu Property Co

Yuexiu Property Co, incorporated in Hong Kong and listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, operates in the real estate sector with activities that include property development and investment. Through subsidiaries such as Guangzhou Haiyiyuan, the group also engages in specialized projects including the provision of elderly-care services using leased properties in Guangzhou.
